perturbation.original_text: 7. Warranties 7.1 Performance Warranty. Todos warrants that for a period of one (1) year from the date of delivery of each Product to the Reseller, the Product, except for those components that have a shorter expiration date as set forth on Exhibit A, shall perform substantially in accordance with the Product's documentation and specifications, and shall be free from all defects in materials, manufacture, and workmanship. Todos shall correct or repair any reported non-conformity or defect, or replace the non-conforming Product with a Product that conforms to this warranty. 7.2 Non-Infringement. Todos represents and warrants to the Reseller that Todos has full right to produce and sell the Products as contemplated by this Agreement, and that the Reseller's exercise of the resale rights granted herein will not violate any copyright, patent, or other proprietary right of any third party. 7.3 EXCEPT FOR THE EXPRESS WARRANTIES STATED IN THIS SECTION 7, TODOS DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL WARRANTIES, INLCUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION OF MERCHANTABLE QUALITY, MERCHANTABILITY, DURABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. NO REPRESENTATION OR OTHER AFFIRMATION OF FACT, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO STATEMENTS REGARDING PERFORMANCE OF THE PRODUCTS, WHICH IS NOT CONTAINED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SHALL BE DEEMED TO BE A WARRANTY BY TODOS.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Insurance. Each party shall carry appropriate and commercially reasonable amounts of insurance adequate for the activities detailed in this Agreement, as well as sufficient levels of all legally mandated insurance, if any. 7.3 EXCEPT FOR THE EXPRESS WARRANTIES STATED IN THIS SECTION 7, TODOS DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL WARRANTIES, INLCUDING ANY IMPLIED WARRANTY OR CONDITION OF MERCHANTABLE QUALITY, MERCHANTABILITY, DURABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. NO REPRESENTATION OR OTHER AFFIRMATION OF FACT, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO STATEMENTS REGARDING PERFORMANCE OF THE PRODUCTS, WHICH IS NOT CONTAINED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SHALL BE DEEMED TO BE A WARRANTY BY TODOS.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the warranty disclaimer to the 'Insurance' section, which typically deals with coverage for risks and liabilities, makes it less conspicuous and harder to find for the Reseller. A warranty disclaimer is directly related to the seller's guarantees about the product's quality and performance and is expected in a section dedicated to warranties.
perturbation.law_explanation: Israeli Sale Law, 1968, Section 16 implies that a seller must disclose any defects or non-conformities in the goods being sold. While a complete disclaimer may not directly violate this section, its placement in an unexpected section like 'Insurance' could be argued as an attempt to obscure this disclosure, thereby weakening the seller's defense against claims related to undisclosed defects. The court might view this placement as a lack of good faith in informing the buyer about the potential limitations of the product.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the warranty disclaimer to the insurance section makes it less conspicuous and harder to find. This falls under 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction,' where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The law explanation supports this by stating the placement in an unexpected section could be argued as an attempt to obscure this disclosure. Therefore, the change creates a legal contradiction because it undermines the accessibility and clarity of a legally significant disclaimer.
